verdictRationale: Two P0 security defects discovered in PR #2280: AC#6 token invalidation violation (IMP-046) and nested impersonation via URL bypass (IMP-013 URL branch). Test cases are well-formed; failures are real feature defects.
changedModules: vc-frontend storefront only — no backend module versions changed

bugReports.title: Impersonation token NOT invalidated server-side after Revert — AC#6 violation
Nested impersonation via URL bypasses CanImpersonate check

proposedInvariants.status: FALSIFIED — do NOT promote until BUG-1 fixed
proposedInvariants.rule: Upon revert-impersonation (backToOperator()), the impersonation access_token MUST be revoked server-side; subsequent API calls with the revoked token MUST return HTTP 401.

changedFindings.fix: Update label assertion from 'Revert back to own account' to dynamic pattern 'Back to {operatorFullName}'
Change Continue button assertion to OK button
Same as AUTH-028 (button label is OK, not Continue)

blockedFindings.reason: Test data ambiguity — invited-status member shows 'Login on behalf' action; needs backend clarification or contact-only seed user
Out of time budget — cross-tab broadcast verification deferred
Out of time budget — i18n revert string verification deferred
